requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype. Inflammatory acne supports hyperandrogenism and changes androgen testing threshold.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).
requiredExam.technique: Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When androgen excess or PCOS is possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Inflammatory acne supports hyperandrogenism and changes androgen testing threshold.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect acne distribution is supportive for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

dispositionRules.rationale: Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).
dispositionRules.diagnostic_target: Pregnancy, fertility intent, rapid virilization, tumor-range hormones, or pituitary symptoms changes medication safety, imaging urgency, and referral pathway.: result or bedside context that changes treatment intensity, monitoring, referral, or disposition for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).
dispositionRules.management_change: Pregnancy, fertility intent, rapid virilization, tumor-range hormones, or pituitary symptoms changes medication safety, imaging urgency, and referral pathway.
dispositionRules.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
dispositionRules.limitations: This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).

dispositionRules.label: Biochemical hypogonadism, POI, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, or thyroid disease changes counseling, bone/cardiometabolic prevention, and treatment selection.

reference_values: Adults: PCOS generally requires 2 of 3 Rotterdam features after excluding mimics: ovulatory dysfunction, hyperandrogenism, PCOM/elevated AMH.
Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
